shows: Naturalisation list: 'Schroder, Rudolf Bruno, Germany, 7th August 1914, Banker, 35 Park Street, London' (oath of allegiance 3 days after Britain entered the war)
Ordered to be wound up; in liquidation under Trading with the Enemy
Board of Trade order under Trading with the Enemy Amendment Act 1916
Official Receiver as Controller of the Dresdner Bank (London) Branch
Wartime partner-disclosure list: 'J. HENRY SCHRODER & CO', Baron Bruno Schroder
1917 partner list still names Baron Bruno Schroder; firm operating
1910 partnership notice: Sir John Henry Wm Schroder, Baron Bruno Schroder, Frank Tiarks
Eric Hambro made Commander of a British order (Ministry of Information)
Enemy notice is 'Rudolf von Goldschmidt-Rothschild', a different person, not the British house
1856 notice: firm 'Fruhling and Goschen', Austin Friars, City of London
